title: action.rolled_back.v1
description: SPEC I (Wave I, AMD-03) — Action Platform envelope: a previously executed action was ROLLED BACK (side effect reversed) by its executor adapter. Registered in Apicurio as brain/action.rolled_back.v1 (JSON Schema artifact under FULL_TRANSITIVE, AMD-03 R1). Partition key = brand_id (I-S01). SCAFFOLD ONLY — no adapter implements rollback yet (ExecutorPort.rollback is NotImplemented, supportsRollback=false). GOVERNANCE: a working rollback for an executor is one of the three preconditions ('auto' policy version + holdout + rollback) before autonomous execution of that executor is permitted; this envelope is the audited reversal record. rollback_ref echoes the action.executed.v1 execution_ref that was reversed. PII (I-S02): rolled_back_by is an auth-principal id, reason is free text with no PII.

brand_id.description: Tenant key — brand_id-first (I-S01). Kafka partition key.

action_id.description: The action being rolled back (same id as its action.executed.v1). Idempotency key = (brand_id, action_id).

executor.description: The executor adapter that performed the reversal (matches an @brain/action-core ExecutorPort name).

execution_mode.description: Mode of the original execution being reversed.

rollback_ref.description: The action.executed.v1 execution_ref (executor-returned handle) that this rollback reversed — the reversal audit link.

rolled_back_by.description: OPTIONAL auth-principal id of whoever triggered the rollback (human operator or an automated safety controller); never raw PII.

reason.description: OPTIONAL free-text reason for the reversal (no PII by contract, I-S02).

holdout_group.description: OPTIONAL incrementality cohort label (preserved across the lifecycle).

rolled_back_at.description: OPTIONAL ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the reversal.
